summary: This lecture discusses fundamental Islamic jurisprudential principles regarding the permissibility of worship versus customs. The speaker explains that worship is impermissible by default unless specifically instructed by Allah and the Prophet, whereas customs and daily activities are permissible by default except where explicitly prohibited. The lecture then applies these principles to fasting during prohibited times, specifically the two days of Eid, citing evidence from hadith collections about the prohibition of fasting on Eid days when the Prophet Muhammad forbade such fasting.
suggested_title: The Principles of Worship vs. Customs: Permissibility and the Days When Fasting is Prohibited
hadith_chapter: The prohibition of fasting on Eid days
catchy_title: Why You Can't Fast on Eid: The Worship Rule
